rationale.prehire: Self-served, token-linked screening that ranks candidates on a composite — built for volume.
rationale.ac_selection: Behavioural evidence from exercises + assessors gives the most defensible selection signal.
rationale.ac_development: A development centre surfaces per-competency gaps and feeds an individual development plan.
rationale.ac_succession: Scores feed the Talent Map's 9-box and the succession-readiness pipeline.
rationale.reflect: Multi-rater frequency ratings against observable leadership behaviours.
rationale.fluent: An indicative CEFR placement across the four language skills.
rationale.technical: Domain-knowledge items with a documented cut-score for a certifiable result.
rationale.ara_org: Pillar-based maturity across the organization, with peer benchmarks.
rationale.ara_personal: Four-factor individual AI readiness, self-served or consultant-issued.
rationale.psychometric: Cognitive ability + Big-Five personality — the foundational 'potential' layer that predicts the behavioural competencies.

cmd.title: Technical Assessment Command
cmd.intro: Track the certification pipeline across every domain — from item bank to issued credentials. A domain certifies only once its bank is SME-approved and a cut-score is set.
cmd.pipelineSub: Build the bank, set the standard, certify the domain, then assess and credential — continuously.

console.title: SME item-review console
console.intro: A technical_proficiency credential is only issued from items reviewed and approved here, scored against a documented cut-score. AI drafts the items; a subject-matter expert approves them. Below the bar, the assessment still runs — but stays indicative, with no credential.
console.readinessNote: A domain becomes certifiable once its approved-item count reaches the cut-score's minimum. Until then, takers get an honest indicative band only.

runner.intro: An indicative assessment of technical proficiency across VIFM's finance & management domains — finance, treasury, accounting, banking, analytics and more. The third capability pillar, beside behavioural competencies and English. For development, not certification.
runner.aiWarnBody: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for real, domain-specific items — a placeholder set is shown otherwise.

bridge.title: Behavioural bridge — {{domain}}
bridge.intro: Which behavioural competencies (of the AC 38) a strong result in this technical domain enables. A measured result surfaces as a chip on each competency below, on the learner's skills page.
bridge.noneMapped: No competencies mapped yet. Add one below.
bridge.keyNote: The domain key {{key}} is immutable (it's the foreign key used everywhere). Only display names change here.

sme.rationalePh: Why this standard is defensible for this domain…

sme.fillArabicHint: Translate any bank items missing an Arabic question or options
sme.certNote: {{approved}} approved · needs {{min}} to certify ({{pct}}% to pass).
sme.noItems: No items yet. Use “Generate drafts” above to seed the bank, then review each one.
sme.question: Question

take.errBuild: Couldn't build the assessment. Please try again.
take.chooseIntro: A short, indicative assessment of your technical proficiency in one domain. About 8 questions.
take.chooseFunctionIntro: Choose your competency area, then the function or role within it. We assemble a deep, multi-skill assessment across the skills that function actually requires — not a handful of generic questions.
take.selectFunctionSub: Select the function or role to be assessed on.
take.mixStart: Start combined assessment — {{skills}} skills across {{n}} functions
take.mixNeedMore: Pick at least {{min}} skills (from any competencies) to start.
take.mixHint: Tap a function to open its skills — pick one, some, or all. Then keep going across functions and competencies to build your custom assessment.
take.showDomains: Or assess a broad domain instead
take.domainsHint: Broad single-domain screeners — shorter and less role-specific than a function.
take.answerAll: Answer all {{n}} questions. Your answer key is graded on the server.
take.bandShort: short test — read the band, not the point
take.adaptiveNote: The test adapts to your answers and stops once it's confident.
take.credNote: You scored {{pct}}%, at or above the {{cut}}% cut-score for this domain. Your credential is verifiable below.
take.belowNote: You scored {{pct}}%. This domain certifies at {{cut}}%. Keep developing and re-take the certified assessment — no credential is issued below the standard.
take.discCertified: Certified assessment. Every item was reviewed and approved by a subject-matter expert and scored against a documented cut-score, server-side. A credential is issued only at or above the standard. Levels: {{levels}}.
take.discIndicative: Indicative only. This short, {{kind}} assessment signals technical proficiency for development — it is not a certified qualification and issues no credential. Items should be human-reviewed and calibrated before any high-stakes use. Levels: {{levels}}.

prehire.footerConfidential: VIFM Pre-Hire · Confidential — for VIFM and engaged clients only.
prehire.lead: Configurable screening funnels — competency quiz, English placement, and an AI behavioural interview — with a weighted composite, adverse-impact monitoring, and an audit trail. The score is a signal; a person always decides.
prehire.noneBody: Create a requisition to start screening candidates for a client role.
prehire.newReqIntro: Define the role, pick the screening stages, and set cut-scores.

prehire.noCandidates: No candidates yet. Add one above to send them through the screening pipeline.
prehire.signalNote: Scores are a screening signal. A human reviews and makes the hiring decision — the pipeline never auto-rejects.
prehire.reportNotConfigured: Email isn't set up on this server, so nothing was sent to {{to}}. Download the report and send it yourself, or configure email on the server.
prehire.reportsNotConfigured: Email isn't set up on this server — no reports were sent. Configure email on the server (or download each report and send it manually).

prehire.recipientHint: Add the client's email above and click Save to enable “Send to client”.
prehire.addedEmailed: Candidate added and invitation emailed to {{email}}.

prehire.fairApplyTail: to enable demographic monitoring and the audit trail.
prehire.noDisclosed: No disclosed data in the analysis pool yet.
prehire.underpowered: Sample too small for a reliable ratio (aim for ≥30 in the pool and ≥5 per group) — treat as indicative only.
prehire.gJobBody: Stages and cut-scores are configured from the role's competency profile — measuring what the job requires.
prehire.gHumanBody: The composite is an advisory screening signal. It never auto-rejects; a person records the decision with a job-related reason.
prehire.gConsentBody: Each candidate consents before any data is collected, and every significant action is written to an immutable log (below).
prehire.gDemoBody: Self-ID is voluntary, separate from the assessment, and used only in aggregate for the adverse-impact check below — never per individual.
prehire.poolLine: · pool {{pool}} · selected {{selected}}. A group flags when its selection rate falls below 80% of the highest-selected group. This is a monitoring signal for review, not proof of bias.

exercise.phScenario: Background story and context for the exercise scenario. Describe the fictional company, the participant's role, and the situation they will face...

engTech.title: Technical Certification
engTech.intro: Assign technical domains to certify this cohort. Each candidate takes the assigned test; a passing certified sitting issues a verifiable credential.

techFn.title: Functions
techFn.intro: The job-level units of technical assessment. Use the standard finance library, or import a job description to derive a custom function — its required technical skills.
techFn.importIntro: Paste the JD text or upload a PDF/TXT. We derive the technical skills it requires — reusing existing skills where possible and proposing new ones.

techFn.composeIntro: When a role spans several functions — e.g. one employee responsible for Accounts Receivable AND Financial Reporting, or finance mixed with data analytics — pick the functions below and we merge their skill blueprints into one custom assessment.
techFn.composeEmpty: Tick one or more functions above — from any competency — to build the combined blueprint.
techFn.composeCreated: Custom assessment created — find it in the library below to run or certify it.
techFn.composeHint: Untick any skill the client doesn't need. Skills shared between functions are merged once, and any already-approved item banks for these skills are reused automatically.

cert.certIntro: Build the SME-approved item bank that lets this function issue a verifiable credential. Each blueprint skill needs at least the floor of approved items.

cert.calibrateHint: Write each item's IRT difficulty from response data (adaptive-testing groundwork).

candidateConsent.title: Consent & Data Protection
candidateConsent.noticeIntro: In accordance with the UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 on Personal Data Protection, the Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL), and the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), we are required to obtain your explicit consent before collecting and processing your personal data.
candidateConsent.noticeCollect: Virginia Institute of Finance and Management (VIFM) will collect and process the following data during the assessment:
candidateConsent.collectItem4: Audio/video recordings (if applicable for virtual sessions)
candidateConsent.noticeRetention: Your data will be retained for a maximum of 2 years from the assessment date unless otherwise agreed contractually. You have the right to access, correct, or request deletion of your data at any time.
candidateConsent.readConfirm: I confirm I have read and understood the notice above.
candidateConsent.dataConsent: I consent to the collection and processing of my personal data as described above for the purpose of the assessment center evaluation. I understand my rights under applicable data protection laws.

candidateAssessments.title: Your Assessments

candidateAssessments.important1: Each exercise is designed to assess specific competencies relevant to the target role. Please arrive prepared and on time for each session.

araConsultant.list_subtitle: Your Compass engagements across GCC clients.
araConsultant.list_empty_body: Create your first engagement to invite respondents, gather evidence, and begin Phase 1 discovery.
araConsultant.new_step1_intro_before: Stage drives the pillars in scope, the report length, and whether this is a complimentary lead-in or a fee-based engagement. You can't change it later - start a new assessment if scope grows. Looking for the Personal Snapshot? It has its own self-served flow at
araConsultant.new_signpost_title: Add a workforce-readiness layer to any stage
araConsultant.new_signpost_body: Every Department, Division, or Enterprise assessment can optionally include the four-factor individual layer alongside the org-side pillar items - gives you a per-respondent personal readiness rollup plus a cohort dashboard. You'll find the toggle on Step 2 once you pick a stage.
araConsultant.new_templates_intro: Curated bundles tuned to common GCC engagement patterns. Picking one pre-fills the stage, sector, and pillar weights - you can still edit everything in Step 2.
araConsultant.new_step2_subtitle: Region and sector are set at creation and never change.
araConsultant.new_no_active_version_before: No active question bank version. Respondents will have no questions to answer until one is published.
araConsultant.new_scope_help_department: e.g. Risk Management, IT, Human Resources
araConsultant.new_scope_help_division: e.g. Retail Banking, Treasury, Operations Division
araConsultant.new_scope_help_enterprise: Optional - leave blank for an organisation-wide assessment.
araConsultant.new_pillars_help: Different sectors prioritise different pillars. Defaults are recommended; swap any to match the client's focus.
araConsultant.new_sandbox_help: Test or demo data. Emails redirect to the sandbox address. Excluded from analytics. Can be bulk-deleted.
araConsultant.agentic_body: As organisations move from using AI to letting autonomous agents take actions on their behalf, the question shifts from capability to control. This layer assesses six governance dimensions - agent accountability, human oversight, failure-mode awareness, tool/data access control, autonomy calibration, and auditability.
araConsultant.agentic_toggle_label: Add the Agentic-AI Readiness layer to this engagement
araConsultant.agentic_toggle_help: Every respondent will also answer 18 agentic-readiness items (~4–6 min) alongside their pillar questions, and the client PDF gains an Agentic-AI Readiness section with a cohort score per dimension.
araConsultant.individual_headline_before: Pillars measure what the organisation has built.
araConsultant.individual_headline_accent: Factors measure how its people behave.
araConsultant.individual_body_before: A department's realised AI readiness is the intersection of both - the org can score 5 / 5 on Data and Strategy, but if the workforce scores 2 / 5 on AI Working Practice the value still doesn't land. Adding the individual layer is the one place the consultant report can say
araConsultant.individual_body_quote: “your strategy is solid; the gap is adoption”
araConsultant.individual_toggle_label: Add the workforce readiness layer to this engagement
araConsultant.individual_toggle_help: Every respondent will also answer the four-factor personal AI readiness items (AI Sense-Check · AI Working Practice · AI Collaboration · AI Adaptive Mindset) alongside their pillar questions. The consultant assessment dashboard gains a cohort-rollup card, the client PDF gains a Workforce AI Readiness section, and each respondent gets their own personal results page after submitting.
araConsultant.individual_tier_help: Snapshot is reliable for directional reads (per-factor α ≈ 0.78). Deep-dive is research-grade (α ≈ 0.85+) and recommended when individual-level decisions follow the report.
araConsultant.pillar_picker_intro: Pick the {{n}} pillars this assessment will score. Defaults to the recommended set; swap any pillar to match the client's focus.
araConsultant.pillar_picker_warning: Pick exactly {{n}} pillars to continue. Click any tile to add or remove - when you're at the cap, clicking a new one swaps in.

araAssessmentDetail.sandbox_body: Emails redirect; data is excluded from analytics.
araAssessmentDetail.unfreeze_desc: Respondents will be able to update their answers again, and scores will continue to recalculate as responses come in.
araAssessmentDetail.freeze_desc: Takes a snapshot of current scores for the report. Respondents can no longer change answers until you unfreeze. You can always unfreeze and refreeze.
araAssessmentDetail.reopen_desc: Moves back to Phase 1 and unfreezes scores. Respondents can answer again.
araAssessmentDetail.archive_desc: The assessment is closed to further activity. Generated reports are kept. You can reopen later. Archived assessments are purged after 3 years per retention policy.
araAssessmentDetail.shadow_ai_desc: Signals suggest employees may be using public AI tools without formal governance. Investigate in Phase 2.
araAssessmentDetail.shadow_ai_mentions: AI tool mentions in open-text answers
araAssessmentDetail.gap_detector_desc: Respondents on the same pillar disagree significantly. Investigate in the Phase 2 workshop.
araAssessmentDetail.live_scores_desc: Recalculates on every respondent answer. Freeze before generating the report.
araAssessmentDetail.pillar_weights_desc: Adjust how each pillar contributes to the overall score. Weights must sum to 100. Default is equal (12.5% × 8).
araAssessmentDetail.guide_empty_a: No Layer 2 guide questions exist on this version of the bank. Admin can add them at
araAssessmentDetail.guide_desc: Layer 2 questions for the Phase 2 workshop. Use these to dig deeper than the Layer 1 self-assessment and uncover the “why” behind each score.
araAssessmentDetail.capability_plan_desc: Per-pillar maturity gap (target 4) × course relevance. Use during the Phase 2 workshop to anchor the development conversation in concrete VIFM offerings.
araAssessmentDetail.capability_plan_empty: No course recommendations yet - either no pillar scores have been computed, the org is at or above target maturity (level 4) on all pillars, or the catalogue doesn't yet cover the relevant pillars.
araAssessmentDetail.upskilling_desc: AI orders the recommended programmes into a foundation-first sequence, with a rationale, milestone, and target outcome per stage. Generate on demand for the Phase 2 conversation.
araAssessmentDetail.open_client_dashboard_title: Open the read-only cohort dashboard - share this URL with the client sponsor
araAssessmentDetail.workforce_desc_a: Cohort-level four-factor readiness rolled up across every respondent who answered the individual layer. Per-person breakdown shows who is pulling the cohort up or down. The
araAssessmentDetail.workforce_desc_b: link above opens a read-only summary (no respondent identities) at a public URL you can share with engagement sponsors.
araAssessmentDetail.workforce_empty: No individual-layer responses yet. Once respondents complete the assessment, their four-factor scores roll up here.
araAssessmentDetail.development_demand: Development demand · % of cohort below target (4)
araAssessmentDetail.workforce_training_title: Workforce training plan - based on cohort factor gaps
araAssessmentDetail.workforce_training_desc: VIFM courses ranked by where the cohort scores below target across the four individual readiness factors. Driver chips show which factor's gap pulled each course into the list.
araAssessmentDetail.workforce_training_empty: No recommendations yet - cohort means are at or above target across all four factors, or the catalogue doesn't yet cover the relevant capabilities.
araAssessmentDetail.agentic_desc_b: to autonomous AI agents - six governance dimensions that extend the Governance and Model Management pillars. Rolled up across every respondent who answered the agentic layer.
araAssessmentDetail.agentic_empty: No agentic-layer responses yet. Once respondents complete the assessment, their six-dimension scores roll up here.
araAssessmentDetail.control_gaps: Control gaps · % of cohort below target (4)
araAssessmentDetail.notes_desc_a: Observations from the Deep Dive workshop. Toggle
araAssessmentDetail.notes_desc_b: to surface a note in the final report; otherwise it stays internal.
araAssessmentDetail.note_placeholder: What did you observe in Phase 2?
araAssessmentDetail.compliance_desc: {{region}} frameworks applicable to your sector. Auto-derived from answers; override when you have direct evidence.
araAssessmentDetail.compliance_empty: No frameworks apply to this region/sector combination.
araAssessmentDetail.yoy_compatible: Comparing against {{year}} assessment (same major question bank version).
araAssessmentDetail.usecase_desc: AI initiatives inventoried by respondents - stage, risk, and business value.
araAssessmentDetail.materials_desc: Documents and links submitted by respondents as evidence.
araAssessmentDetail.validity_desc: Statistical signals on each respondent's answer pattern. Used by the consultant to decide whether to validate input in Phase 2 - never auto-rejects responses.
araAssessmentDetail.respondents_desc: Stakeholders who will complete their assigned pillar sections. Each receives a unique access link - no account required.
araAssessmentDetail.respondents_empty: No respondents yet. Add one below.
araAssessmentDetail.add_respondent_desc: Assign one or more pillars. Respondent sees only their assigned sections.
araAssessmentDetail.assign_pillars_help: Only pillars selected as in-scope at assessment creation appear here ({{n}} of 8). Out-of-scope pillars are hidden so a respondent can't be assigned to a pillar the report won't render.
araAssessmentDetail.individual_layer_note: Note: this assessment has the individual readiness layer enabled. Respondents will also receive the four-factor items in addition to their pillar questions.
araAssessmentDetail.individual_only_label: Individual layer only - skip pillar questions
araAssessmentDetail.individual_only_help: For workforce-wide invitees who shouldn't answer org-side pillar questions (e.g. inviting 50 line employees while only 8 senior leaders cover the pillars). Respondent gets only the four-factor items. Ignore the pillar checkboxes above.
araAssessmentDetail.bulk_import_required: Paste a CSV with a header row to invite multiple respondents at once. Required columns:
araAssessmentDetail.bulk_import_individual_b: (yes/no) skips pillar questions and serves only the four-factor items.
araAssessmentDetail.bulk_skipped: Existing emails on this assessment are skipped automatically. Pillar IDs:
araAssessmentDetail.reassess_dialog_body_year: Creates a new draft assessment for next year, seeded from this {{year}} baseline. Region, sector, stage, scope, default language, and pillar weights carry over. The new assessment will use the currently active question bank version.
araAssessmentDetail.reassess_dialog_body: Creates a new draft assessment for next year, seeded from this baseline. Region, sector, stage, scope, default language, and pillar weights carry over. The new assessment will use the currently active question bank version.
araAssessmentDetail.reassess_carry_respondents: Carry respondents over (each gets a fresh access link; invitations are not auto-sent).
araAssessmentDetail.invite_title_sandbox: Send invitation (sandbox - recipient overridden to SANDBOX_EMAIL_REDIRECT)
araAssessmentDetail.dd_page_subtitle: 48-item, research-grade individual AI readiness assessment for a paying HR client's named employee. ~10 minutes for the respondent. Each of the four VIFM factors gets 12 items - roughly double the reliability of the free 24-item snapshot.
araAssessmentDetail.dd_measured_desc: Four VIFM factors mapped to the AC behavioural framework. The deep-dive includes the snapshot items plus 6 additional items per factor that probe sub-facets the snapshot can't sample.
araAssessmentDetail.dd_issue_card_desc: On submit, we create the assessment, generate a respondent access URL, and return the magic link so you can copy it into the email or invite that fits your client's workflow. The 16-min reminder cadence and other respondent affordances (auto-save, language toggle) are inherited from the standard respondent flow.
araAssessmentDetail.dd_toast_issued: Deep-dive issued - copy the link below
araAssessmentDetail.dd_toast_copy_failed: Couldn't copy - please select and copy manually
araAssessmentDetail.dd_issued_help: Send this magic link to the employee. They'll land directly in the respondent flow with the 48 individual-factor items. On completion, the system emails them the results URL + PDF.
araAssessmentDetail.dd_org_name_placeholder: e.g. ACME Bank - keeps deep-dives associated with the paying client

araAdminData.ret_cron_step4_suffix: → Run workflow, to verify end-to-end before waiting for the next 03:00 UTC fire.
araAdminData.ret_cron_closing: Once both env vars are set, the GitHub Actions workflow attaches the bearer header on every scheduled invocation; this banner flips green on the next page load.

araReport.cohort_subtitle: Aggregate AI-readiness across the cohort. Read-only view.
araReport.cohort_empty_state: No respondents have completed the individual readiness layer yet. This dashboard will populate as the cohort responds.
araReport.cohort_score_placeholder: Cohort score will appear here once respondents complete the assessment.
araReport.cohort_readiness_by_factor_desc: The cohort's aggregate score on each of the four VIFM personal AI-readiness factors. Tones reflect how far the average sits from target (4 / 5).
araReport.cohort_development_demand_desc: Percentage of respondents currently scoring below the target of 4 on each factor. The longer the bar, the more candidates would benefit from development on that factor.
araReport.cohort_methodology_body: The cohort score is the mean of every respondent's factor average. Respondents who haven't answered a factor are excluded from that factor's mean (rather than counted as 0). Item development, content validity, reliability planning, and limitations are documented in the
araReport.cohort_footer_note: This dashboard is a read-only summary intended for engagement sponsors. Per-respondent scores and identity are not surfaced here - ask your VIFM consultant for the consultant-side view if needed.
araReport.gantt_empty: No recommendations to plot - populate Phase 2 consultant notes per pillar to generate a roadmap.
araReport.compliance_none: No applicable regulatory frameworks for this region/sector.

configurator.heading: How many competencies do you need to assess?
configurator.intro: The right tier and exercise mix is driven by competency count, not a fixed package. Move the slider to see the recommended shape.
configurator.frameworkNote: Drawn from VIFM's 38-competency framework. Range: 1–12 typical; custom frameworks supported on the Partnership tier.

rec1.rationale: Two competencies are best served by one interview plus one situational exercise - enough triangulation without over-engineering.

rec2.rationale: 3-4 competencies fit comfortably inside a half-day with three observation surfaces, hitting the AC requirement of each competency observed in at least 2 exercises.

rec3.rationale: 5-6 competencies need a fourth surface to keep each competency observed across multiple exercises with sufficient evidence.

rec4.duration: Full-day · ~7 hours per candidate · cohort scheduling
rec4.rationale: 7-8 competencies require business simulations across both individual and group surfaces. This is where cohort scheduling (3-6 candidates per session) becomes the efficient delivery shape.

rec5.rationale: 9-10 competencies cross the threshold where bespoke exercises become worth commissioning. Programme tier is the natural fit.

rec6.duration: 2 days · custom framework + dedicated assessor team
rec6.rationale: Beyond 10 competencies, you're assessing the whole executive profile. The Partnership tier covers a custom framework, dedicated assessors, and quarterly trajectory reviews.

roadmap.heroBody: From a client brief to a released candidate report - the full nine-step journey, every component that powers it, and the standards it's anchored to.
roadmap.journeyIntro: Every Assessment Centre engagement follows the same arc. Each step maps to a concrete portal screen, a defined role, and a documented artefact.
roadmap.exerciseIntro: Every exercise has a defined timing structure (instructions / preparation / meeting), a participant briefing, an assessor guide, and a competency-to-exercise matrix slot.
roadmap.complianceIntro: Every Assessment Centre engagement runs against five published standards. Audit trail on every rating decision, immutable observation log, candidate consent captured before any data collection.
roadmap.footerTagline: The Big-4-calibre talent decision platform for the GCC.

journey.discoverBody: Define target role, hiring or development objective, candidate population, success criteria, and timeline. Capture the competency profile up front.
journey.calibrateBody: Map each in-scope competency to the exercises that will measure it. Every competency lands in at least two exercises - cross-validation by design.
journey.designBody: Build or adapt the in-basket, role play, group exercise, case study, oral presentation, and CBI guides. Bilingual EN / AR materials for GCC clients.
journey.recruitBody: Add candidates to the engagement (bulk CSV or one-by-one), assign trained assessors, send branded invite emails with consent, schedule the AC dates.
journey.observeBody: Assessors run the AC and capture observations against the BARS rubric. Each observation tagged to a competency with positive/negative indicators.
journey.integrateBody: Each assessor consolidates their evidence in the integration worksheet, then the wash-up engine drives multi-rater consensus with live Realtime collaboration.
journey.decideBody: Wash-up produces an OAR (1-5) and a recommendation: Ready Now / Ready with Development / Not Ready. ICC computed across the assessor pool.
journey.reportBody: Per-candidate report with cover, executive summary, competency detail (strengths + development areas), and tiered development recommendations.
journey.followupBody: Reports released to candidates, cohort analytics shared with the client, and (for Programme / Partnership tiers) a quarterly cohort review with the C-suite.

acFluent.heroDescription: Reading and Listening are AI-generated and auto-scored; Writing and Speaking are scored live against the CEFR criteria by our AI examiner - the part IELTS and TOEFL pay human examiners for.
